text:
| case_law_summary__miller_mcfarlane__financial_remedy | financial_remedy | What unrepresented people most commonly misunderstand about this case — e.g. whether they conflate the sharing principle with a guaranteed 50/50 split, or whether they understand the compensation strand at all |
| case_law_summary__re_h_n__child_arrangements | child_arrangements | What unrepresented people most commonly misunderstand about how domestic abuse allegations are handled in children proceedings — e.g. whether they believe the criminal standard of proof applies, or whether they understand the difference between a fact-finding hearing and the welfare hearing that follows it |
| document_explanation__form_a__financial_remedy | financial_remedy | What unrepresented people typically get wrong about Form A — e.g. not understanding that it must follow the MIAM, filing it without understanding what type of order to seek, not appreciating that filing starts a timetable with strict deadlines, or not knowing the court fee |
| document_explanation__d8__divorce | divorce | What unrepresented people typically get wrong about the D8 — e.g. believing that filing the D8 addresses financial matters, not knowing the difference between sole and joint applications, confusion about when the 20-week period starts, losing or not having the marriage certificate, or not knowing a certified copy can be obtained |
| document_explanation__consent_order__financial_remedy | financial_remedy | What unrepresented people typically get wrong about consent orders — e.g. believing an informal written agreement is binding, not knowing about the D81 requirement, submitting a draft order with ambiguous or incomplete terms, or not including a clean break clause where appropriate |
| document_explanation__position_statement__cross_cutting | cross_cutting | What unrepresented people typically get wrong about position statements — e.g. not knowing they are expected to file one, confusing a position statement with a witness statement, writing one that is too long and too detailed, filing late, or not serving on the other party |
| document_explanation__witness_statement__cross_cutting | cross_cutting | What unrepresented people typically get wrong about witness statements — e.g. not numbering paragraphs, including argument and opinion rather than evidence, not attaching or labelling exhibits, misunderstanding the statement of truth, not staying within any page or word limit directed by the court, or including hearsay without identifying the source |
